name: Workspace Region Stack
description: Internal dashboard/workspace layout primitive for stacking multiple panels, grids, or empty states inside a single labeled section without relying on d-section to create child spacing.

standard.description: Comfortable vertical stack for grouping multiple surfaces or data regions inside one section label.

slots.region-child: Any child panel, grid, card group, or empty state that should maintain a stable vertical gap from the next child

compact.description: Slightly tighter stack for denser operator workspaces while preserving explicit child spacing.

slots.region-child: Compact child panel, grid, card group, or empty state

visual_brief: A quiet structural primitive, not a visible widget. It exists to preserve clear x- and y-axis rhythm between multiple blocks that belong inside the same dashboard section. Think of it as the section's internal cadence: label first, then a measured stack of grids, surfaces, forms, summaries, or empty states. It should prevent collisions without adding its own decorative treatment.

responsive.mobile: Maintain explicit vertical gaps between children even when grids collapse or cards stack. Never depend on child margin hacks.
responsive.tablet: Preserve the same region ordering and stable spacing while allowing children to reflow responsively.
responsive.desktop: Use a comfortable but restrained vertical cadence so dense dashboard surfaces still feel organized.

layout_hints.ownership: This pattern owns vertical spacing between sibling regions inside one section. Parent d-section still owns outer section padding and section-to-section rhythm.
layout_hints.section_role: Use this when a single section label introduces multiple children such as a KPI grid followed by a summary card, or a filter panel followed by results.
layout_hints.anti_pattern: Do not place multiple unwrapped sibling panels directly under a d-section and expect d-section to create child spacing. That responsibility belongs here.
